identity.canonical_title: TRAWINISTIC WINDING NUMBER Canonical Statutory Formula: ν_T

theory.canonical_statement: [ID 856] TRAWINISTIC WINDING NUMBER Canonical Statutory Formula: $\nu_T = \frac{1}{4\pi^2} \iint_{T^2} F \wedge F$ Formal Technical Dissertation: A topological invariant where $F$ is the curvature 2-form. $\nu_T$ is an integer identifying the topological charge of the system, essential for the stability of the Trawin Zero Point. Source: [11]
theory.technical_interpretation: TRAWINISTIC WINDING NUMBER Canonical Statutory Formula: ν_T is an algebraic definition, written ν_T = (1)/(4π^2) iint_T^2 F wedge F. It is an algebraic definition expressing the quantity directly in terms of the variables on its right-hand side. It is built from π, T, F, defining ν_T. Within the Trawin Zero Point registry it serves as one element of the framework's mathematical narrative.
